title: How Motors Spin?
subtitle: Understanding Motor Motion Through Demonstration
overview: Students learn how motors spin through teacher demonstrations and simple activities. Students compare a spinning DC motor with a paper windmill moved by air to understand motion and rotation.
keyTopics: Comparing motor spinning with wind force

concept.title: How Motors Spin
concept.content: A DC motor changes electrical energy into movement. Electricity flows into the motor and creates force that spins the motor shaft. Students compare this spinning motion by blowing air on a paper windmill to understand how force can create rotation.

activity.title: Paper Windmill and Motor Comparison
steps: Teacher shows DC motor spinning using battery power
Students blow air on paper windmill
Compare motor spinning and wind force spinning

quiz.q: What energy does a DC motor use?
quiz.explanation: DC motors use electrical energy to create movement.

quiz.q: What happens when students blow on a paper windmill?
quiz.explanation: Air force makes the windmill spin.
Motors convert electrical energy into movement.
